name: prep-readiness
title: Prep Readiness
purpose: Machine-enforced readiness checks that gate the prep phase. Answers: 'Do we have everything we need ready to make the full change?'

requirements.title: Requirement Smell Detection
requirements.body: The validator must scan requirement titles and bodies in spec deltas for language patterns that indicate ambiguity, subjectivity, or unmeasurable claims. These patterns are advisory warnings — not blockers — because smell heuristics have false-positive risk.

scenarios.title: Subjective language detected as warning
given: A change delta whose requirement title contains 'easy' or 'simple' or 'nice'
then: A SMELL_SUBJECTIVE warning is returned
The gate is NOT blocked (severity is warning, not error)

scenarios.title: Ambiguous scope detected as warning
given: A requirement title containing 'etc' or 'and/or' or 'various'
then: A SMELL_AMBIGUOUS warning is returned

scenarios.title: Superlative language detected as warning
given: A requirement title containing 'best' or 'fastest' or 'always'
then: A SMELL_SUPERLATIVE warning is returned

scenarios.title: Negative phrasing detected as warning
given: A requirement title containing 'not' or 'never' or 'without'
then: A SMELL_NEGATIVE warning is returned

scenarios.title: Totality claim detected as warning
given: A requirement title containing 'all' or 'every' or 'none'
then: A SMELL_TOTALITY warning is returned

scenarios.title: Clean requirement produces no smell warnings
given: A requirement with a specific, measurable, positively-phrased title
then: No smell warnings are returned for that requirement

scenarios.title: No smell checks run when change has no deltas
given: A change with tasks but no spec deltas (bug-fix scenario)
then: No smell issues are emitted (nothing to check)

requirements.title: Scenario Adequacy Enforcement
requirements.body: Requirements added via deltas must have at least one scenario defined. This is a must-level failure because requirements without any scenarios cannot be tested or validated.

scenarios.title: Requirement with no scenarios is a must-failure
given: A change delta that adds a requirement with no scenarios array or an empty scenarios array
then: A SCENARIO_MISSING issue with severity 'error' is returned
The prep gate is blocked

scenarios.title: Requirement with at least one scenario passes
given: A change delta that adds a requirement with one or more scenarios
then: No SCENARIO_MISSING error is returned for that requirement

scenarios.title: Only happy-path scenario on non-trivial requirement is a warning
given: A requirement with exactly one scenario whose title contains only 'happy path' language and the requirement body suggests error/edge conditions exist
then: A SCENARIO_INADEQUATE warning is returned (advisory only)

scenarios.title: Bug-fix change with no deltas is not penalized
given: A change with tasks but zero spec deltas
then: No SCENARIO_MISSING errors are emitted

requirements.title: TDD Inversion Detection
requirements.body: Prep-readiness owns enforcement of TDD inversion at planning time. A task graph where a same-scope test task is blocked_by an implementation task violates the inline TDD contract. Detection uses metadata.tdd_intent first (values: inline, separate_verification, not_applicable), falling back to title-based heuristics for legacy tasks without metadata. Tasks with tdd_intent 'separate_verification' or 'not_applicable' are exempt from inversion checks. On the title-heuristic (metadata-less) path the inversion finding is advisory (severity 'warning') and does NOT by itself block the prep gate: a title regex must not solely own a hard gate-block (P33 structural-correctness). The authoritative gate-block for missing or invalid TDD intent is TASK_TDD_INTENT_MISSING (rq-PR006tdi). Canonical TDD semantics and classifier expectations are defined by tdd-contract/rq-TDD005inv and rq-TDD002sep; this requirement defines the prep gate readiness behavior and failure surface.

scenarios.title: Test task blocked by impl task is an advisory warning (title heuristic)
given: A task without metadata.tdd_intent whose title matches test-task heuristics
That task has a blocked_by dependency on another task whose title matches impl-task heuristics
then: A TASK_TDD_INVERSION issue with severity 'warning' is returned (advisory)
The prep gate is NOT blocked by the inversion finding alone
The authoritative gate-block for missing or invalid TDD intent is TASK_TDD_INTENT_MISSING

scenarios.title: Task with tdd_intent 'inline' prevents false positives on test-like titles
given: A task with metadata.tdd_intent set to 'inline'
The task title contains test-like language such as 'test-first' or 'spec'
That task has a blocked_by dependency on an implementation task
then: No TASK_TDD_INVERSION error is returned
The metadata classification takes precedence over title heuristics

scenarios.title: Task with tdd_intent 'separate_verification' is accepted
given: A task with metadata.tdd_intent set to 'separate_verification'
That task has a blocked_by dependency on an implementation task
then: No TASK_TDD_INVERSION error is returned

scenarios.title: Task with tdd_intent 'not_applicable' is exempt
given: A task with metadata.tdd_intent set to 'not_applicable'
then: No TASK_TDD_INVERSION error is returned

scenarios.title: Legacy tasks without metadata fall back to title heuristics
given: A task without metadata.tdd_intent set
The task title contains TDD-significant keywords
then: Title-based regex heuristics (isTestTask/isImplTask) are used for classification
The task is checked for TDD inversion using the heuristic result

scenarios.title: Invalid tdd_intent values fall back to title heuristics
given: A task with metadata.tdd_intent set to an unrecognized value (not inline, separate_verification, or not_applicable)
then: The invalid value is ignored
Title-based regex heuristics are used as fallback

scenarios.title: Tasks with no TDD-significant keywords are not flagged
given: Tasks about docs, chores, or releases with no test/impl naming pattern and no metadata.tdd_intent
then: No TASK_TDD_INVERSION error is returned

scenarios.title: Orphan task warning
given: A task with no deps and which is not a dependency of any other task
then: A TASK_ORPHAN warning is returned (advisory only)

requirements.title: Cross-Repo Routing Completeness
requirements.body: Tasks that have one of target_repo or target_path set but not both have incomplete routing metadata. This creates ambiguity about where the task should be executed and must be flagged as a must-level failure.

scenarios.title: Task with target_repo but missing target_path is a must-failure
given: A task with target_repo set but target_path absent
then: A CROSS_REPO_MISSING_METADATA issue with severity 'error' is returned
The prep gate is blocked

scenarios.title: Task with target_path but missing target_repo is a must-failure
given: A task with target_path set but target_repo absent
then: A CROSS_REPO_MISSING_METADATA issue with severity 'error' is returned

scenarios.title: Task with both fields set passes routing check
given: A task with both target_repo and target_path set
then: No CROSS_REPO_MISSING_METADATA error is returned for that task

scenarios.title: Task with neither field set passes routing check
given: A task with neither target_repo nor target_path set (local task)
then: No CROSS_REPO_MISSING_METADATA error is returned

scenarios.title: Task with repo hint in title but no routing metadata warns
given: A task title containing '[backend]' or '~/dev/' but no target_repo/target_path
then: A CROSS_REPO_HINT_UNROUTED warning is returned (advisory only)

requirements.title: Prep Gate Readiness Enforcement
requirements.body: The adv_gate_complete tool for the prep gate must run all prep-readiness checks before marking the gate done. Must-level failures block gate completion; warnings produce advisory output but do not block.

scenarios.title: Gate blocked when must-failures exist
given: A change with at least one prep-readiness must-failure
then: The gate is NOT marked done
The response includes readinessFailures array with check IDs and remediation hints
The response has success: false or error field

scenarios.title: Gate succeeds with only warnings
given: A change with no must-failures but one or more warnings
then: The gate IS marked done
The response includes readinessWarnings array (advisory)

scenarios.title: Gate succeeds cleanly with no issues
given: A change that passes all prep-readiness checks with no failures and no warnings
then: The gate IS marked done
No readinessFailures or readinessWarnings in response

scenarios.title: Non-planning gates are not affected
given: adv_gate_complete called for proposal, discovery, design, execution, acceptance, or release
then: No prep-readiness checks are run; behavior is unchanged from baseline

requirements.title: TDD Intent Assignment at Prep Finalization
requirements.body: During prep finalization, every non-cancelled task MUST have an explicit metadata.tdd_intent value assigned (inline | separate_verification | not_applicable). The prep gate MUST verify that all tasks have tdd_intent set and reject gate completion when any task lacks it. This ensures TDD classification decisions are made deliberately at planning time, not deferred to implementation where they may be skipped or forgotten.

scenarios.title: Task without tdd_intent blocks prep gate
given: A change with at least one non-cancelled task that has no metadata.tdd_intent set
then: A TASK_TDD_INTENT_MISSING issue with severity 'error' is returned
The prep gate is blocked

scenarios.title: Task with valid tdd_intent passes check
given: A change where all non-cancelled tasks have metadata.tdd_intent set to one of: inline, separate_verification, not_applicable
then: No TASK_TDD_INTENT_MISSING errors are returned

scenarios.title: Cancelled tasks are excluded from the check
given: A change with both cancelled and non-cancelled tasks
Only the cancelled tasks lack metadata.tdd_intent
then: No TASK_TDD_INTENT_MISSING errors are returned
Cancelled tasks are not considered

scenarios.title: Feature flag advisory mode downgrades severity
given: Project config has tdd_enforcement set to 'advisory'
A change with a task missing metadata.tdd_intent
then: A TASK_TDD_INTENT_MISSING issue with severity 'warning' is returned
The prep gate is NOT blocked

scenarios.title: Feature flag off mode skips the check entirely
given: Project config has tdd_enforcement set to 'off'
A change with tasks missing metadata.tdd_intent
then: No TASK_TDD_INTENT_MISSING issues are emitted
The check is skipped entirely

scenarios.title: Invalid tdd_intent value is treated as missing
given: A task with metadata.tdd_intent set to an unrecognized value (not inline, separate_verification, or not_applicable)
then: A TASK_TDD_INTENT_MISSING error is returned for that task
The error message indicates the invalid value

requirements.title: Critical Operations Coverage
requirements.body: Planning readiness MUST verify that every required-critical contract item (priority required, class critical-ops) has at least one implementing or verifying task in the task graph. A required-critical item without task coverage represents an unplanned obligation and blocks the prep gate. Items with an explicit notRequiredReason are exempt from coverage checks.

scenarios.title: Required-critical item with task coverage passes
given: A change contract contains a required-critical item
At least one task in the task graph references that item via contract_refs.implements or contract_refs.verifies
then: No CRITICAL_OPS_MISSING_COVERAGE error is returned for that item

scenarios.title: Required-critical item without task coverage fails
given: A change contract contains a required-critical item
No task references that item via contract_refs
then: A CRITICAL_OPS_MISSING_COVERAGE issue with severity 'error' is returned
The prep gate is blocked

scenarios.title: Required-critical item with notRequiredReason passes
given: A change contract contains a required-critical item
The item has an explicit notRequiredReason set
No task references that item via contract_refs
then: No CRITICAL_OPS_MISSING_COVERAGE error is returned for that item

requirements.title: Task Ordering Provisional Advisory
requirements.body: For tasks with metadata.tdd_intent='inline' that will be subject to rq-TDD009seq ordering enforcement at completion time, the prep-readiness validator SHOULD emit a TASK_ORDERING_PROVISIONAL advisory (warning severity) reminding the agent that a red→green test-run sequence must be recorded via adv_run_test before task completion. This advisory is non-blocking — it surfaces the new ordering requirement without gating the prep gate. The advisory is informational: it does not check whether prior test runs exist (those are validated at task-completion time by the workflow).

scenarios.title: Inline task gets ordering provisional advisory
given: A task with metadata.tdd_intent='inline'
The task is part of a change subject to rq-TDD009seq enforcement
then: A TASK_ORDERING_PROVISIONAL warning MAY be returned for the task
The prep gate is NOT blocked (severity is warning)

scenarios.title: Non-inline tasks do not get ordering advisory
given: A task with metadata.tdd_intent='not_applicable' or 'separate_verification'
then: No TASK_ORDERING_PROVISIONAL advisory is returned for the task

requirements.title: Non-Code Task Evidence Policy Readiness
requirements.body: Prep readiness MUST fail when a non-cancelled non-code deliverable task lacks a valid machine-readable evidence policy or uses metadata.tdd_intent='not_applicable' without a task evidence-policy rationale. The check MUST apply to docs, research, approval, ops, and verification tasks as applicable while preserving existing inline TDD enforcement for code tasks.

scenarios.title: Non-code task missing evidence policy blocks prep
given: A non-cancelled task has type research or docs
The task has no evidence_policy and no bounded evidence-policy rationale
then: A non-code evidence readiness error is returned
The planning gate remains blocked until the task has a valid evidence policy or bounded not_applicable rationale

scenarios.title: Non-code task with valid evidence policy passes readiness
given: A non-cancelled research task has metadata.tdd_intent='not_applicable'
The task has evidence_policy='source_citation' and contract_refs to approved criteria
then: No non-code evidence readiness error is returned for that task
The task remains eligible for review-matrix proof instead of red/green TDD proof
